Tests the deletion of function arguments and checks if they become undefined.

# Purpose
This code defines a function [`foo`](<#pebble-firmware/src/fw/vendor/jerryscript/tests/jerry-test-suite/13/13-012foo>) that checks if all elements in its `arguments` object can be deleted. The function iterates over each argument, attempts to delete it, and verifies if the deletion was successful by checking if the type of the argument is `undefined`. The function returns `true` if all arguments are successfully deleted, otherwise it returns `false`. An `assert` statement tests the [`foo`](<#pebble-firmware/src/fw/vendor/jerryscript/tests/jerry-test-suite/13/13-012foo>) function with a set of arguments, expecting the result to be `true`. This code provides narrow functionality, focusing on the behavior of the `delete` operator on function arguments.

### foo<!-- {{#callable:pebble-firmware/src/fw/vendor/jerryscript/tests/jerry-test-suite/13/13-012.foo}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../../../src/fw/vendor/jerryscript/tests/jerry-test-suite/13/13-012.js#L15>)

Attempts to delete all arguments passed to it and returns a boolean indicating if all deletions were successful.
- **Inputs**:
    - `a`: The first argument to be deleted.
    - `b`: The second argument to be deleted.
    - `c`: The third argument to be deleted.
    - `d`: The fourth argument to be deleted.
- **Control Flow**:
    - Initialize a variable `deleted` to `true`.
    - Iterate over each argument using a `for` loop.
    - Delete each argument using the `delete` operator.
    - Update `deleted` to `true` only if the current argument is `undefined` after deletion.
    - Return the value of `deleted`.
- **Output**: A boolean value indicating whether all arguments were successfully deleted and are `undefined`.
